item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't seem to fit the description well. The SCP isn't described as spiky and the image doesn't have "14 sheets of stained glass". And most importantly, the previous image really feels like it's giving off anomalous light unlike this one.
This one does looks interesting as well, but maybe there'd be a different SCP it suits better.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Geograph
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: 'Sapphire Star' blown glass sculpture by Dale Chihuly at Kew Gardens
Author: Rod Allday
Release year: 2019

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoting because I found it quite interesting. It's quite scary if you think about it because one day they might not be able to make a light brighter than the light the SCP emits.
Though, why don't they just destroy it then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I expect that the implication of the WW2 incident is that the sculpture has proven indestructable by any means the Foundation has been able to bring to bear, and any attempt simply makes things worse - or that's assumed to be the case because what set it off unstoppably increasing in brightness was a direct hit from a high explosive Allied bomb?

Seeing as it's been recontained/pacified before in the distant past, maybe exposing it to excessive amounts of peace and loving happiness (something that the Foundation is probably very bad at) might be able to calm it down again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I recommend reading this story after reading this skip and it's companion piece "Stars". It's the aftermath of the destruction of Elrich by the Fallen Star.

Also, I'm gonna assume that the wielders of SCP-1591 were the remnants of the Kingdom of Seva since they were the biggest rivals to Elrich.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Hey man, with all the proposals we have to do, not all of them are going to be winners.

:P

In all seriousness this is a much better set of crosslinks. Lets go with that.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
suggests a potential shared origin

I not a fan of the link, but have an issue with this in particular. This is implied to have a grander, mythic origin in both the endnote and the linked tale; while the linked article is, well, a flipping disco ball.

I say we link this to another sun, like, literally SCP-2975 'Another Sun'. I think it's thematically closer to this than 1969. Something like:

5/19/1941: several documents relating to SCP-1591 were recovered from a location inhabited by members of the Serpent's Hand.

to

5/19/1941: several documents relating to SCP-1591 were recovered by Mobile Task Force Sigma-3 ("Bibliographers"), from a location inhabited by members of the Serpent's hand.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below (see Shaggydredlocks post below). Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

While this article is already crosslinked to the Site-77 dossier, and to that Wanderers Library tale, there is room here, I feel, for another potential crosslinks. Up thread it was mentioned that this SCP is similar to SCP-1969 with regard to both anomalies effects, and their unknown make. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1591 constantly produces light with gradually increasing brightness and intensity1. Any surface illuminated by SCP-1591 will appear to become inconsistently transparent, and if not removed any affected matter will disappear from observable space. Non-solid matter that makes contact with light produced by SCP-1591 will begin to rapidly decrease in temperature until it takes on a solid form. SCP-1591 is immune to its own effect.

To this:

SCP-1591 constantly produces light with gradually increasing brightness and intensity2. Any surface illuminated by SCP-1591 will appear to become inconsistently transparent, and if not removed any affected matter will disappear from observable space. Non-solid matter that makes contact with light produced by SCP-1591 will begin to rapidly decrease in temperature until it takes on a solid form. SCP-1591 is immune to its own effect3.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Currently 10,000,230,450 lx
2. Currently 10,000,230,450 lx
3. Similarity in make and effect between SCP-1591 and SCP-1969 suggests a potential shared origin for the two anomalies. Investigation is ongoing.

item.title: Solution for 682?
item.content:encoded: If SCP 1591 eventually destroys any organic matter, and the foundation wants to destroy 682 as soon as possible, yet are currently unable to do, why not try 1591?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a legit star?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that they're saying that the Foundation is experiencing the same thing that other past civilizations (presumably) experienced; they're desperately trying to fend off a god, and failing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First thought: believe in a Smiling God.

Second thought: We have become the Moabites. We are learning how it was for the Amalekites. They faced extinction at the hands of Adonai. They died for His purpose, they fell as we were falling, they are afraid as we are afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even know what is going on in here, and the mere fact that it's upvoted so highly makes me want to know, even if I don't particularly like what is already here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if that would be a bad thing…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wow I really enjoyed this!
Only the picture doesn't look much like the description.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Congrats! Great article here!

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: yaaaaaaaaaaay
About fucking time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Challenge accepted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So then 682 does what it always does and adapts, producing the same light at an even greater intensity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two possible reasons:

Out-of-universe: It might work, and then we'd lose possibly the second best-known article on the site.

In-universe: He doesn't just not die. He adapts. Imagine a giant omnicidal lizard with glorious vaporization rays of death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… let me get this straight. When the light touches any living thing, they start to disappear, then try to flee, at which point they just get instantly vaporized. And we haven't tried using this on 682 yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fine for me, and it is localized. And Roget, you might want to delete the lower res duplicate image in Files.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Somehow, the image is dead for me. Maybe localising it will fix it?

Edit: Welp, looks like it's actually my own problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These featured skips never fail to impress me. I always go to the newest ones, because i thought i had gone through the old ones i like and then… These appear, once again being awesome. I should press "random" more.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I never noticed the partial people in the picture before (I'm guessing this was, like, a long exposure). Almost think they could be incorporated into the article somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a higher resolution version of the image, obtained from the chinese site. Thanks China!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always disliked the idea of linking (in the literal sense) SCP articles to tales. Too bad I already downvoted this for other reasons, or I would have for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said elsewhere: It's the exact same universe (allowing for multi-canon as per usual).

The Library was created right here on this site. It's been referenced many times on this site. It has an entire group of interest (the Serpent's Hand) attached to it.

This is like downvoting SCP-173 because you're strongly against direct carryovers from Containment Breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, just saying, I thought the WL, GOC, and SCP wikis all shared a universe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is not a direct carryover. Maybe you should try to even glance at the other thing before assuming that it's the exact same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even if everyone else are big fans of WL, I'm still strongly against direct carryovers from other fictional universes into the SCP world.

-1 (although it hurt me to do this, because you wrote a fucking great scip)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god damn it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then why isn't that in the article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When SCP-1591 comes into contact with a solid object composed of matter

Uh, do you mean SCP-1591's light?

Overall this works pretty well for me, though I can't say I totally 'get' the history behind it. Not sure if that's a writing issue or not though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a long, proud tradition of writing essays about Marxism and getting things wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I can say is, this is significantly more impressive than my own idea for that image. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same problem here.. also there's things like

ability to create speech

Ability to verbally communicate, if anything.

Also,

The intensity of the light produced by SCP-1591 does not decrease over distance.

Even though it's anomalous, I dislike this, because it isn't quite lampshaded enough. I mean… the reason why the intensity should decrease over distance is geometry - the amount of photons given out is the same, but for a greater distance between you and the object, the spatial angle (basically, a proportion of a sphere's surface, usually measured in steradians) you occupy is a lot smaller.
This'd suggest there are photons given out beyond the surface of the object, the only reasonable explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HAhahaha, YES.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of that boss from Kirby 64.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And that's why I'm a biology major, not a physics major.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah. I tried googling "highest lux light" and such, but I wasn't finding anything that high so I assumed there wasn't any.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Serpent's Hand didn't contain this, they "contained" the story about it (and still do).

And it's entirely possible that they had nothing to do with it actually, since they're only a small part of the Library.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, well, I stand corrected. Didn't even occur to me that there might be motion overlap.

On a only semi-related note: a lot of those intallations could be solid scp fuel if you could get a photo without tourists in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As should be apparent in the third photograph on this page, the object is in fact a cube— it appears to be a less regular shape because it is rotating. If the SCP were intended to be cuboid, I feel like that would have been mentioned in the description. You are quite right about the glass sheets, though. Perhaps the key would be to clarify their arrangement?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't unsee it now. It's Ramiel with an ever growing AT field. Oh, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It takes a little while to erase things, so the measurements would have to be taken quickly and with disposable equipment, but it could be done.

In the absence of weight, mass can be determined by inertia. You push/pull on it and note how much force it takes to move it a certain distance at a certain speed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perspective is fooling your eye, and possibly a slight mis-read of the description.

A six-sided figure would be a cube. While it's a bit too complex for me to say what shape that picture really is, it's definitely more multi-faceted than a cube.

But more importantly, the star is "…surrounded by 14 sheets of stained glass.", not necessarily of equal size and/or arranged in a regular quadradecahedron (is that the right spelling?).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We've had lights which can produce over 30 billion lux since the 40's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm kind of wondering how the Foundation was able to determine the mass of an object that both levitates and erases from existence everything its light falls upon. This does feel like a Keter we can't stop though, good job. I have to wonder how we have lights that are over 10 billion lx though…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was based on this, which may clear your confusion somewhat.

And really? Aliens?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I adore this, but the image does not match the description. The "star" would appear to be encased in a six-sided figure, not a 14-sided one. Is there a reason for that?

+1 anyway. Excellent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A keter scp with an Xk tag? Finally? Great. I thought I won't live to see a new one :)

I wonder: Did serpent's hand know how to contain the SCP properly? Cause from what I read, it seems it's becoming a much bigger threat once it got into the Foundation's hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
""Every 60 meters, photosensitive broadcasting units" - in a circle? In a sphere? In a straight line? What if the breach happens between (laterally) two units?"

I don't see this as a problem when I read this article.

Isn't it implied that the object's effect grows and grows, so eventually the next sensor in the line would pick it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have these same problems, except I'm personally taking a more "blow it up" approach. I mean, come on, it's made of glass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
solid object composed of matter

Isn't this redundant?

I liked "Stars" better, but this is interesting and well-written. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"angels"

*Looks at shape*

HOLY SHIT IT'S RAMIEL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but I feel like there's some kind of twist I'm missing. I'm too lazy to read linked material, though, so in my head this is a weapon sent by "angels" to clean evil from the earth, which grows in dangerousness as the total amount of badness in the world increases, and it earns a hearty +1.

My further interpretation, for what it's worth, is that all the nasty stuff the foundation has to do (including interfering with this and other scps) qualifies them as evil in the morality this thing operates by, and that the thing is growing so dangerous because it's trying harder and harder to eliminate the foundation specifically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the person who wrote the note is a Jewish person witnessing Germany invading, but I have an even stronger feeling that I'm entirely incorrect.

I know it has something to do with some big war, that much is obvious. It's a weapon granted by aliens(?) to hopefully end conflict on the planet Earth.

It gets more powerful with every act of violence committed?
